title: viola mandshurica var. mandshurica
intro: Viola mandshurica var. mandshurica, also known as the Chinese violet or Manchurian violet, is a perennial plant in the violet family, native to China and Korea. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and improving respiratory health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and bronchitis.
